assumptions: 'Set up your agent to pull' = the step where the user pastes the play-prompt into their MCP client so the agent starts polling for turns (today's seat-connect / loop-running state).
The nav label may keep 'connected once' semantics while the join gate keeps 'live now' — modeled as a parameter on the resolver (e.g. require_live), not two separate ladders.
'Per-provider MCP connection' is a CONCEPTUAL unit derived over connection_providers toggles; physical storage is unchanged.
discovery.summary: Rich prior conversation settled the design; carrying 3 explicit assumptions. Cross-cutting across 6 files with intentional behavior changes — full FF path forced (size heuristic mis-reads the not-yet-implemented 0-line diff as trivial).
non_goals: No new module or subsystem — consolidate onto connection_health.py + nav_context.py only.
No DB schema or migration change; storage stays one-connection-per-user + connection_providers per-provider toggles.
No change to the MCP OAuth bridge or per-user connection creation mechanics.
Do not revisit strategy-first ordering — agent-before-connection stays.
Do not redesign the seat-hold / connect-countdown UX; only its 'what is missing' decision routes through the resolver.
acceptance_criteria: spec.md tables how all 5 entry points (nav CTA, /play, auth.py post-login redirect, agents_create destination, web_player._join_setup_redirect) decide the next step today — naming the exact predicate each uses — and the agreed unified definition for each gate.

connection_health.py exposes ONE provider-capability function returning a tri-state {NO_MCP_CONNECTION, CONNECTED_NOT_LIVE, LIVE}; the ~6 existing predicates are re-expressed over it or explicitly documented as retained wrappers.
nav_context.py exposes resolve_onboarding_state(user, intent) -> (stage, next_url); all 5 entry points call it instead of re-deriving the ladder.
Every behavior change at a site that disagrees today is enumerated and intentional, with a before/after note.
Tests cover the tri-state and the resolver ladder for each stage transition; Preflight Gate green.

checklist.goal: One intent-driven onboarding pipeline whose 'what is my next step' logic is centralized on EXISTING modules (no new module): a single provider-capability tri-state in app/engine/connection_health.py, and a shared resolve_onboarding_state() promoted from compute_nav_cta in app/routes/nav_context.py that every entry point calls.
checklist.audience: Signed-in users getting an AI agent into a game (esp. the 'signed in, no provider connected' path), and the developers who keep hitting drift bugs because each onboarding entry point re-derives the ladder differently.
checklist.constraints: nav_context.py lives in app/routes/ and would own a broadened resolver; an optional rename to onboarding_state.py is cosmetic, not architectural.

annotations.reason: Spec reviews accepted via manual independent sub-agent adversarial pass (codex/gemini CLIs unavailable in this remote container; --fallback hits a telemetry RecursionError). Both lenses reconciled to accepted; all findings folded into spec v2.
